title: Credits — Modern Mythology
desc: End-of-volume credits theme. The first pad of title_theme returning slower, lower, resolved.

title: Modern Mythology
desc: The theme that plays at the beginning of everything.

title: The Diner at the Crossroads
desc: The diner. The stranger. The choice that starts everything.

title: The Underworld
desc: Intercut. Dancing. Loud music.

title: The Missing Link
desc: Bus depot. Rain. No clock.

title: Dream States
desc: Faust's bedroom. The room behind the room.

title: Driving Around · Night
desc: EXT. Judgement Day. Headlights, then more headlights.

title: Faust III
desc: An echo of Sharp's bass but warmer — the same world from another mouth. Faust's signature.

title: The Choice (resolved)
desc: Released when Vol 1's central choice is committed. A held breath finally let out.

title: The Painting
desc: A slow oil-tone pad. The painting that knows you saw it.

title: The Pharmacy Mirror
desc: Pharmacy fluorescent buzz. The mirror that doesn't quite reflect right.

title: Sharp
desc: Low bass swell, basement amp buzz. Sharp's signature, played whenever Sharp shows up.

title: The Stranger
desc: A single sustained tone in the upper register. The Stranger arrives at the booth.

title: Crossroads · Title
desc: Volume 1 title card. Warm pad + radio hush. The full deck has not yet been dealt.

title: Small Wood Variations
desc: The sound of trees that have been standing longer than the town.

title: Briar Falls · After Dark
desc: Pine-wind drone, owl on a far ridge. Late at Briar Falls.

title: The Graveyard
desc: A single low pad, no field-recording layer. The quietest track in Vol 2.

title: Lena
desc: Acoustic guitar single-note phrase, repeated, mid-period Mark Hollis register. Lena's signature.

title: Seagash, Closed
desc: Released when the Seagash mystery's last interlude resolves.

title: Rest Stop · Wind
desc: Briar Falls. Mid-nineties. Twelve hours from anywhere.

title: The Siren of Seagash
desc: A history, with interludes.

title: Station Omega-14
desc: The hum of life support and the silence of deep space.

title: Antonio
desc: Single piano figure on the upbeat, returning each time the call doesn't connect.

title: The Call Drops
desc: Antonio mid-sentence, the line going hollow. A single note holding for too long.

title: Decompression
desc: Released when Vol 3's choice resolves. The pressure equalizes.

title: Tobor
desc: Metallic resonator + held vacuum hum. The robot's signature.

title: Five AM, No One Around
desc: The city before it remembers you're not supposed to be there.

title: Dante D'Ambrosio
desc: A bowed double-bass note held until the bow leaves the string. The father.

title: The Standoff (resolved)
desc: Released when the Vol 4 standoff resolves. The room finally lets you sit down.

title: Nicola
desc: A short three-note descending figure on a detuned upright piano. Nicola's signature.

title: Five AM Strings
desc: A single sustained note from a Lo-fi taped cello at the climax of the standoff.

title: Alberto · Sunglasses Indoors
desc: Bowed cello single note, dampened. The grieving son.

title: D'Ambrosio's at Dawn
desc: Open 24 hours. Technically.

title: Anya · The Recording
desc: Found-tape hiss + an almost-melody just under the noise floor. Anya's signature.

title: Sysop · ember.ash.rest.bbs
desc: Modem handshake + low fluorescent buzz.

title: The Bus Kid
desc: A bright wood-block tap + a smile of brass. The helper's signature.

title: Cicadas at Dusk
desc: Chapter II — The High Priestess.

title: Elicia · Tape Reel
desc: Cassette-spool noise + a soft sustained voice-tone (no vocal). Elicia's signature.

title: Frasier · Notebook
desc: The pen scratch behind the warehouse drone — distinct from the chapter ambient.

title: Twenty-Four Hours (reversed)
desc: Released the first time you lose any Tarot Gauntlet scenario.

title: The Leap (won)
desc: Released the first time you win any Tarot Gauntlet scenario.

title: John Frank
desc: An apron-tied phrase: a single mid-range pad, a slow heartbeat below. John's signature.

title: The Line Cook
desc: Heat-vent hum + a single brass bell strike at the start. The cook's signature.

title: Mackenzie · Receiver
desc: Phone-line static + a single piano left-hand figure.

title: Maya · The Trick
desc: Music-box loop, ten seconds long, slightly varied each pass.

title: The Bindle
desc: Released the first time you successfully assemble the Bindle in THE LEAP.

title: Noon Room Room
desc: Tarot Gauntlet · Lunch Service jukebox B-side. Lake Charles accordion, brass-mic'd dance-floor sound.

title: Static Bloom
desc: Chapter III — The Empress.

title: The Man at the Bar
desc: A cold sine pad about a half-step out of tune with everything around it.

title: Cathedral of Rust and Code
desc: Chapter I — The Magician.

title: Where the Bar Used to Be
desc: Tarot Gauntlet · Evening Service jukebox B-side. Solo after-hours piano recorded in the diner in 1979.

title: The Bird-Watcher
desc: Chair creak + binocular-strap rustle + a slow patient exhale. The watcher's signature.

title: Blow Out the Candles
desc: Tarot Gauntlet · Magician · hard-mode B-side. Full moon over the river, the bank making a noise the bank should not be making, candle hiss, distant rager house-party low end. 11:47 PM.

title: The College Friend
desc: An old laugh, far in the back of the cathedral. The kind that remembers the year you stopped speaking.

title: The Critic
desc: Pen-on-notebook scratch + a single sharp inhale through the nose. No music underneath.

title: Filly (demon)
desc: A young horse's exhale. A hoof on wood, once. Then waiting. The Filly demon's room-tone.

title: Husk (demon)
desc: Hollow corn rasp. Dry rustle. The sound of nothing left inside the case.

title: Moth (demon)
desc: Paper-against-bulb flutter. A glass tap. One high whistling overtone that doesn't resolve.

title: Starling (demon)
desc: Many small wings. No melody. The whole flock turning the same instant.

title: Steamboat (demon)
desc: A small paddle-wheel micro-loop in the room — not the river version. This is the one Frasier is building on the workbench, and the texture is wrong.

title: Vagrant (demon)
desc: Low rumble + slow shuffle of bags + distant radio static. The Vagrant demon's drift through the room.

title: Weir (demon)
desc: Water under boards. Pressure building behind a thing about to spill.

title: The Drifter
desc: Gravel under boot. A far-away match-strike. The drifter doesn't stay long enough to leave a melody.

title: Drunk Uncle
desc: A baseline-room thump that refuses to sync to anything else playing. A glass on wood. The uncle who arrived and will not be greeted.

title: The Room Sat on Him
desc: Magician finale stinger — generic stagnation fallback. The room is exhausted and all motion has stopped.

title: The Maker Breaks
desc: Magician finale stinger — Doubt at max. Silence under one pad falling a half-step. The last breath before the host closes his eyes for the last time.

title: The Maker Forgets the Make
desc: Magician finale stinger — Stagnation at max. Cassette-deck end-of-tape spool. Lights at full. The cathedral exhales without him.

title: The River Takes the Bank
desc: Magician finale stinger — the bank goes. A building giving up. Slow wood-and-metal collapse rendered as a half-degree sustained slide.

title: The Room Walked Out
desc: Magician finale stinger — three visitors claimed. Everything has dropped out except the Demon's pad, held alone.

title: Midnight, and Behind
desc: Magician finale stinger — midnight overrun, the work undone. The workbench-lamp click off. Then nothing.

title: The Steamboat Sails
desc: Magician finale stinger — the steamboat finished. Paddle wheel turning once though no one is touching it. Then the river bank slumps, far away. Then a held tone, resigned.

title: The Candles Are Lit
desc: Released the first time you light the candles at the workbench in BLOW OUT THE CANDLES. A single sustained match-strike + held flame-hiss + a low pad. The cake is on the workbench.

title: Sinking Feeling
desc: Tarot Gauntlet · Magician · easy-mode B-side. Afternoon sun on the cathedral skylights, low river lap outside, a distant freight horn. The riverside knowing what's coming and not saying.

title: The Paddle Wheel Builds Itself
desc: Magician's doom-clock. A slow paddle-wheel and far steam-whistle that thickens layer by layer across the run, one new element at each steamboat-ladder tier (0 → 6). Frasier hears it building whether he's working on it or not.

title: The Superfan
desc: Too-fast breath. Paper bag. Plastic-cup tap. A loyalty that hasn't read the room.

title: The Twins
desc: Two of the same tone, played milliseconds apart, panned hard left and hard right. Stereo phase. They share patience, they share a theme.

title: Watch Party
desc: Tarot Gauntlet · Magician · medium-mode B-side. TV-room hum, the Pomegranate Hour theme bleeding through one wall, a far laugh from someone who isn't supposed to be in the room. 9:30 PM.

title: Alma · Pleasant Beige
desc: Saccharine corporate-jingle-adjacent two-note motif. Alma's signature.

title: Harmony Creek Estates
desc: Sixty-eight degrees, year-round, by corporate decree.

title: Sixty-Eight, Year-Round
desc: HVAC system + a single sine pad at 68 Hz. Clean. Inhuman. The planned community at rest.

title: The Community (acknowledged)
desc: Released at the Vol 6 turning point.

title: The Planning Office
desc: Office-air recirc + a single fluorescent buzz, no human movement.

title: Smolvud Ambient
desc: The kind of town that knows when you have arrived before you do.

title: Lena's Apartment · Rain
desc: Six o'clock. Four coats on four hooks.

title: Aria
desc: A single hand-bell strike, then a held silence of three seconds, then again. Aria's signature.

title: Bread, Table, Family
desc: Kitchen fire crackle, distant German hymn fragment, table set for nine.

title: Hymn Fragment · From Another Room
desc: A German hymn played on a far piano in another room of the house. Recurs across the family scenes.

title: Marit
desc: An older voice humming a hymn fragment, very close to the mic. (No legible vocal.)

title: Aria Rings the Bell
desc: Released at Vol 7's title moment.

title: Rain on Roof
desc: With two of them left in it the apartment was hers again.

title: Werner
desc: A slow upright piano with the una corda pedal in. The bread-maker's signature.
